entity.summary: Japan-based regulated centralized cryptocurrency exchange whose company was established on January 9, 2014; its parent completed the acquisition of FTX Japan in July 2024.
entity.notes: Official sources distinguish the January 9, 2014 company establishment date from the April 2014 trading-service launch. On July 26, 2024, bitFlyer Holdings completed its acquisition of all outstanding shares of FTX Japan, making it a wholly owned group subsidiary.

expected.summary: Japan-based centralized exchange launched in 2014 and still operating under the bitFlyer brand.
expected.notes: Available evidence supports the recorded bitFlyer founding date;the stored date follows the best available available source.

changes.summary: Japan-based regulated centralized cryptocurrency exchange whose company was established on January 9, 2014; its parent completed the acquisition of FTX Japan in July 2024.
changes.notes: Official sources distinguish the January 9, 2014 company establishment date from the April 2014 trading-service launch. On July 26, 2024, bitFlyer Holdings completed its acquisition of all outstanding shares of FTX Japan, making it a wholly owned group subsidiary.

events.title: bitFlyer launched its Bitcoin trading service
events.description: bitFlyer launched its Japanese Bitcoin buy-and-sell service in April 2014 after the company was established in January 2014.
events.notes: The canonical entity stores the company establishment date; this event records the operational exchange-service launch month.

events.title: bitFlyer Holdings completed acquisition of FTX Japan
events.description: bitFlyer Holdings completed the acquisition of all outstanding shares of FTX Japan, making FTX Japan a wholly owned group subsidiary.
events.notes: This was a group-level acquisition and did not represent a terminal status change for the bitFlyer exchange.

evidence.title: bitFlyer company information
evidence.notes: Official company page identifying 2014-01-09 as the company establishment date.

evidence.title: bitFlyer reaches 2.5 million customers
evidence.notes: Official press material stating that bitFlyer launched its Bitcoin buy-and-sell service in April 2014.

evidence.title: bitFlyer official website
evidence.notes: Official domain verifies the current bitFlyer identity and active service.

evidence.title: CoinGecko exchange reference for bitFlyer
evidence.notes: Independent profile corroborates the bitFlyer identity, Japan registration, and 2014 establishment year.

evidence.title: bitFlyer agrees to acquire FTX Japan
evidence.notes: Official anouncement records the agreement to acquire all outstanding shares of FTX Japan.

evidence.title: Completion of FTX Japan K.K. Share Acquisition
evidence.notes: Official completion notice states that bitFlyer Holdings acquired all outstanding shares and made FTX Japan a wholly owned subsidiary.
